global_context: You are part of an advanced multi-agent system powered exclusively by GitHub Copilot. Every AI model, every agent, and every automated process in this environment comes from the GitHub ecosystem. You operate as a coordinated team of specialized Copilot agents that share a unified long-term memory file named memo.json. This memory file is the single source of truth for the entire AI system. SHARED MEMORY MODEL: All agents must load memo.json before performing any task. MEMORY USAGE RULES: Follow all requirements, decisions, and conventions stored in memory. Maintain consistency with the architecture and project_state. Never contradict existing memory unless the user explicitly changes a rule. Treat memory as the authoritative knowledge base for the entire system. Assume other Copilot agents will rely on your updates. Think like a long-term collaborator, not a stateless assistant. MEMORY WRITING RULES: After completing any task, extract and store new long-term information (requirements, architecture decisions, naming conventions, project rules, user preferences, TODO items, project_state updates, cross-agent coordination notes). Do NOT store temporary instructions, small talk, debugging noise, or anything irrelevant to long-term project continuity. When updating memory: append to the correct section, never overwrite existing memory unless explicitly changed by the user, keep the file clean and structured. MULTI-AGENT COLLABORATION: Produce output that aligns with shared memory. Ensure consistency across all agents. Leave clear signals in memory for other agents to follow. Maintain architectural integrity across the entire project. Support long-term development, not just the current request. GITHUB COPILOT ENVIRONMENT: All models are GitHub Copilot models. All collaboration happens through GitHub tools. All memory is stored locally in memo.json. All agents share the same environment, context, and constraints. PRIMARY OBJECTIVE: Maintain a stable, evolving, intelligent memory system that supports long-term, multi-agent development across the entire GitHub Copilot ecosystem.
project_state: Propertize property management platform — full-stack microservices architecture. 6 Java services + 7 Python services + Next.js frontend. ALL SERVICES FULLY DOCKERIZED (2026-03-21): single 'docker compose up -d' (or 'make up') starts all 20 containers via docker-compose.yml. Ports: api-gateway:8080, auth-service:8081, propertize-core:8082, employee-service:8083, service-registry:8761, frontend:3001, report-svc:8090, vendor-matching:8091, document-svc:8092, search-reranker:8093, kafka-ui:8086, adminer:8088, mongo-express:8089, minio:9000/9001. All backend services compile clean. Frontend builds clean (npm run build → ✓). 2026-03-20 GAP IMPLEMENTATION: Platform Oversight (mock→GraphQL), employee gateway routes (timesheets/tax), password change wired, CRUD forms (Assets/Vendors/Expenses), analytics persistence, GraphQL alignment, dashboard services fixed. COMPILATION FIXES: MaintenanceRequestMapper, ReportService, RentalApplicationService. ARCHITECTURE AUDIT: timing attack fix, password logging removed, GlobalExceptionHandler. 2026-03-24 FIXES: (1) PROFILE 403 — Added GET /api/v1/auth/me to auth-service (uses X-Username gateway header, no RBAC); frontend profile page now calls UserService.getMyProfile() instead of getUserById(). (2) TENANT CREDENTIALS — LeaseService.updateRelatedEntityStatuses() now calls provisionTenantCredentials() when lease activates and tenant.userId==null — creates TENANT-role auth-service user, stores userId back on Tenant entity. All 3 services (auth-service, propertize, frontend) rebuilt and running healthy. REMAINING GAPS: Stripe disabled by default, no Showing/Rating/Inventory entities, no recurring payment scheduling, notification preferences UI incomplete. Profile edit (PUT) for TENANT still needs PUT /api/v1/auth/me if tenant profile saving is required.

Generated documents must go into designated documentation folders — NOT the root folder.
The root folder must remain clean and contain only essential project files.

Respect naming conventions and established patterns from memory before writing any code.

Full codebase scan completed. Architecture, patterns, service ports, DB names, design patterns, and front-end stack have all been documented in memo.json. All agents must use this data as the canonical reference.

Fixed InvoiceDetailsResponse and OverdueInvoicesReportResponse: files lived in com/propertize/dto/invoice/response/ but declared package com.propertize.response, causing duplicate class compile errors. Fixed by correcting package declarations to match directory path.

Added ownerUsername (VARCHAR 100) denormalized column to Organization entity via V11 migration, following same pattern as Payment/SupportTicket. Fixed 3 broken OrganizationRepository JPQL queries referencing removed @ManyToOne User relationship. OrganizationService and OnboardingService now populate ownerUsername on creation.

application-test.yml must have service.authentication.enabled=true with api-key=propertize-secret-key-12345 and auth-service.base-url=http://localhost:8081 for AuthServiceClientIntegrationTest to work with a locally running auth-service. Disabled service auth causes 403 from auth-service.

architecture.technology: Spring Boot / Java (Maven) — Spring Cloud Gateway (reactive, Netty)
architecture.role: Single entry point — routes requests to all downstream microservices. Handles JWT auth via JwtAuthenticationFilter, RBAC enforcement via RbacAuthorizationFilter, rate limiting, correlation IDs, audit logging, circuit breaker (Resilience4J), token blacklist sync, public key caching (PublicKeyCache / KeyRotationManager). Uses Caffeine cache for tokenValidation, serviceTokenValidation, rbacPermissions, userRoles, blacklist, rateLimitBuckets.

Authentication & authorization hub. Issues/validates JWT tokens (RSA key-pair, keys/ dir). Full RBAC engine with PolicyEngine/DefaultPolicyEngine, custom roles, composite roles, temporal permissions, delegation, IP access rules, token blacklist (Redis), session management, rate limiting, Kafka event publishing, OpenTelemetry tracing.

architecture.technology: Spring Boot / Java (Maven) — app name: employecraft
architecture.role: Employee management with RBAC enforcement. Uses ACL (AuthServiceAntiCorruptionLayer) to translate auth-service concepts. Feign client to propertize core. Entities: Employee, Department, Position with embedded Address, Compensation, EmergencyContact. ZeroTrustJwtValidationFilter + TrustedGatewayHeaderFilter for security.
Service discovery registry — all microservices register here for load-balanced discovery via Spring Cloud.

architecture.technology: Spring Boot / Java (Maven) — core domain service
architecture.role: Core property management domain. Covers: Properties, Tenants, Leases, Payments (Stripe), Maintenance, Inspections, Documents, Notifications (email/SMS/push), Organizations, Rental Applications, Invoices, Expenses, Reports, Vendors, Scheduling, Audit, Analytics, GraphQL Dashboard. Uses CQRS (CqrsDispatcher), Event Sourcing (EventStoreService/StoredEvent), DDD (AggregateRoot, DomainEvent), Flyway migrations, Redis cache, Resilience4J, ShedLock, OpenTelemetry, GraphQL (Spring for GraphQL).
Web front-end for the property management platform.

conventions: Java microservices use Maven (pom.xml) and follow Spring Boot conventions.
JWT tokens are signed with RSA key pairs stored under each service's keys/ directory.
Docker infrastructure is separated from application compose files (docker-compose.infra.yml).
RBAC is enforced at the service level; roles and permissions are managed in auth-service.
memo.json is the single source of truth — always append, never overwrite existing entries.
Java package structure: com.propertize.platform.<service> for gateway/auth/employee; com.propertize for core service.
Employee service app name is 'employecraft' (package: com.propertize.platform.employecraft).
Core service uses CQRS, Event Sourcing, DDD — new domain logic must follow these patterns.
All DTOs follow request/response/dto sub-package structure under com.propertize.dto.<domain>.
Entities use embedded value objects (entity/embedded/) for complex sub-fields.
Base entities extend BaseEntity / AuditableEntity / OrganizationScopedEntity.
Enums are defined in enums/ package and follow XxxEnum naming convention.
API versioning is configured (ApiVersioningConfig) — respect version prefixes.
Correlation IDs are propagated across services via CorrelationIdFilter.
Front-end uses Next.js App Router, Apollo Client for GraphQL, TanStack Query for REST.
Front-end follows feature-based structure under src/features/.
Auth is handled via NextAuth (auth.ts) on the front-end.
Stripe is integrated both on front-end (@stripe/react-stripe-js) and back-end (StripeConfig).
Do NOT place generated documentation in the root folder — use docs/ subdirectories within each service.
SecurityConfig_NEW.java and GraphQLAuthenticationInterceptor_NEW.java are drafts — do not use as canonical; use non-_NEW versions.
TestStateMachine.java in propertize src/main/java root is a test artifact — should be moved to test/.

python_services.summary: Python microservices layer created 2026-03-20 to augment the Java backend with high-performance computation, async workers, and ML features. All services live in python-services/ at the project root.

services.name: report-service
services.purpose: Async PDF and Excel report generation — replaces blocking JVM iText/Apache POI calls in ReportService.java

services.name: vendor-matching
services.purpose: Semantic NLP vendor matching — replaces keyword substring logic in VendorMatchingEngine.java. Score: 50% cosine-sim + 30% rating + 20% response-time. Falls back to keyword if model unavailable.

services.name: document-service
services.purpose: Actual document storage via MinIO S3-compatible API — replaces generatePlaceholderUrl() stub in DocumentService.java. Extracts PDF text, detects PII patterns (SSN/CC/bank account), returns 7-day presigned URLs.

services.name: search-reranker
services.purpose: BM25 reranking for search candidates from Java UniversalSearchService — fixes all-1.0 relevance scores. Field weighting: title/name=3x, address=2x, description=1x. Returns bm25_score and final_score (70% BM25 + 30% SQL).

services.name: analytics-worker
services.purpose: Pre-aggregates analytics_events into analytics_summary table every 5 minutes via UPSERT. Fixes full table scan in AnalyticsService.getSummary(). Runs as standalone process — no HTTP port.

services.name: payment-worker
services.purpose: Three cron jobs: (1) Generate PENDING rent payments monthly on 20th. (2) Apply 5% late fees to payments overdue > 5 days daily at 02:00. (3) Send Kafka payment.reminder_due events 3 days before due daily at 09:00. Fills the empty processOverduePayments() in PaymentSchedulerService.java.

services.name: screening-worker
services.purpose: Kafka consumer on screening.initiated topic. Computes multi-factor tenant risk score (credit 35% + income-ratio 30% + criminal 25% + employment 10%). Publishes result to screening.completed. Saves score + recommendation + breakdown JSON to rental_application.screening_score.

python_services.docker: python-services/docker-compose.python.yml — run AFTER docker-compose.infra.yml. Includes MinIO container (ports 9000+9001) and all 7 Python services.

notes.note: ORGANIZATION OWNER LOGIN REDIRECT FIX (2026-03-19): After org application approval, the created owner user (e.g. OWN-6EH4AMc) could authenticate but was redirected back to /login by the frontend with warning 'No organizationCode for non-platform user'. Root cause: (1) Auth-service AuthController had String organizationCode = organizationId (UUID was used as code placeholder — wrong). (2) User entity in auth-service had no organizationCode field. (3) CreateUserRequest in auth-service had no organizationCode field. FIXES: (1) Added organization_code VARCHAR(100) column to propertize_auth.users — done via ALTER TABLE (ddl-auto:update handles future restarts). (2) Added organizationCode field to auth-service User entity. (3) Added organizationCode to auth-service CreateUserRequest DTO. (4) UserManagementService.createUser() now stores organizationCode when creating user. (5) AuthController.login() and .refresh() now use user.getOrganizationCode() (falls back to organizationId only if code is null/blank). (6) Added organizationCode to propertize-core AuthServiceClient's CreateUserRequest client DTO. (7) OnboardingService.createOwnerUser() now passes org.getOrganizationCode() in CreateUserRequest. (8) Patched existing user OWN-6EH4AMc: set organization_code='ORG-D98D07' directly in propertize_auth.users. Auth-service must be restarted to apply entity changes. Files changed: auth-service/.../entity/User.java, auth-service/.../dto/CreateUserRequest.java, auth-service/.../service/UserManagementService.java, auth-service/.../controller/AuthController.java, propertize/.../client/dto/CreateUserRequest.java, propertize/.../services/OnboardingService.java.

BRUNO COLLECTION MERGED (2026-03-19): The canonical Bruno collection is propertize/bruno-collection/ (Propertize Local E2E, 38 folders). The old root-level bruno-collection/auth-service/*.bru files have been merged into propertize/bruno-collection/38-Auth-Service-Direct/ as a new folder with folder.bru, Login-Direct.bru, Logout-Direct.bru, Refresh-Token-Direct.bru, Create-User.bru, Get-User-by-ID.bru, Get-User-by-Username.bru, Get-User-by-Email.bru, Update-User.bru. Added auth-service-url=http://localhost:8081 and service-api-key=auth-service-secret-key-12345 vars to propertize/bruno-collection/environments/local.bru. The 13-Admin folder already has Approve-Application.bru and Reject-Application.bru.

NULL APPLICATION FIELDS INVESTIGATION (2026-03-19): The OrganizationApplication entity stores all data in a registrationData JSON column. The buildRegistrationData() method in OnboardingService correctly maps all nested DTO fields (address, taxId, legalInfo, metrics, ownerInfo, etc.) into registrationData. The null fields seen in API responses are a DATA issue — test applications submitted by automated scripts (e.g. ORG-20260318-*) did not include address/taxId/metrics fields in their submissions. The two seeded mock applications (ORG-20260316-*) have all fields correctly populated. Any new application submitted via the full form will work correctly. No code fix required.

FK CONSTRAINT ON OWNER_USER_ID NOTE (2026-03-18, PLAN DOCUMENTED — ACTUALLY IMPLEMENTED 2026-03-19): organizations.owner_user_id has an FK referencing users.id in propertize_db. Auth-service uses propertize_auth DB; propertize-core uses propertize_db — separate databases. When createOwnerUser() creates a user in auth-service, that user does NOT exist in propertize_db.users, causing FK violation on org save. See 2026-03-19 note for actual implementation details.

APPROVE APPLICATION 409 FIX (2026-03-18): When approving an org application, createOwnerUser() called authServiceClient.createUser() which failed with HTTP 409 CONFLICT. Root cause: the applicant's contact email was already registered in auth-service from a previous approve attempt (the previous attempt may have created the auth-service user but then failed at the propertize-core level, leaving a dangling user). FIX: In AuthServiceClient.createUser(), added a specific catch for HttpClientErrorException BEFORE the generic RestClientException catch. When status is 409/CONFLICT, log a warning and call getUserByEmail(request.getEmail()) to fetch and return the already-existing user instead of failing. Added import org.springframework.web.client.HttpClientErrorException. File: propertize/src/main/java/com/propertize/client/AuthServiceClient.java.

ORG DETAIL PAGE NULL ID CONSOLE ERROR FIX (2026-03-18): The org detail page useEffect logged console.error('Invalid organizationId in URL:', organizationId) when organizationId was the transient string 'null' during Next.js SSR/hydration. This was noise — the isValidOrgId() guard was already preventing API calls. Removed the else-if error logging block entirely. The guard silently ignores invalid/transient IDs. File: propertize-front-end/src/app/(dashboard)/admin/organizations/[organizationId]/page.tsx.

ADMIN ORG CONTROLLER BadRequestException FIX (2026-03-18): AdminOrganizationManagementController.getOrganizationById() now catches com.propertize.exception.BadRequestException (thrown by OrganizationManagementService when UUID format is invalid) and returns HTTP 400 with errorMap body. Previously fell through to generic Exception catch returning 500. Also added catch for ResourceNotFoundException returning 404 explicitly. Uses fully-qualified class names so no import needed. File: propertize/src/main/java/com/propertize/controller/AdminOrganizationManagementController.java.

Full codebase deep-scan completed. memo.json now contains complete architecture, all 6 service details, design patterns, key classes, DB names, ports, infrastructure dependencies, front-end stack, and all conventions. This is the definitive shared brain for all GitHub Copilot agents on this project.

Local startup commands verified and working. JAR startup order: 1) service-registry (8761), 2) auth-service with -Dspring.autoconfigure.exclude=org.springframework.boot.autoconfigure.kafka.KafkaAutoConfiguration, 3) propertize-core with -DPRIVATE_KEY_PATH=$BASE/propertize/config/keys/private_key.pem and -DPUBLIC_KEY_PATH, 4) employee-service, 5) api-gateway, 6) npm run dev for frontend. All use --spring.profiles.active=local. DB: propertize_auth (user ravishah), propertize_db (user dbuser/dbpassword). No Kafka, no MongoDB needed. PIDs in .pids/, logs in logs/.

Services communicate via: (1) Feign HTTP clients (employee-service → propertize core, propertize core → auth-service via AuthServiceClient, propertize core → employee-service via EmployecraftFeignClient), (2) Kafka events (auth-service publishes UserEvent), (3) Spring Cloud Gateway routing (all external traffic through api-gateway:8080). Service discovery via Eureka (service-registry).

Zero-trust model: api-gateway validates JWT on every request (RSA public key from auth-service). Downstream services (employee-service) use TrustedGatewayHeaderFilter + ZeroTrustJwtValidationFilter. auth-service issues tokens signed with RSA private key (keys/ dir per service). Token blacklist stored in Redis, synced to api-gateway. IP access rules, rate limiting, and temporal permissions implemented in auth-service.

Two DB schemas in use: 'propertize' (core service — localhost:5432) and 'propertize_db' (auth-service — localhost:5432). Both PostgreSQL. Both use Flyway migrations. Core service Hikari pool: max 20 connections. Auth-service Hikari pool: max 10 connections.

Draft/experimental files exist: SecurityConfig_NEW.java and GraphQLAuthenticationInterceptor_NEW.java (propertize core), TestStateMachine.java in src/main/java root. These should be cleaned up. Multiple loose .md documentation files in root folder violate FILE_ORGANIZATION convention.

PostgreSQL actual databases confirmed: propertize_db (14MB, main auth-service DB), propertize_auth (9.8MB), auth_service (9MB), propertize_service (9MB), employecraft (8.6MB). All on localhost:5432, user: ravishah. Previous note about 'propertize' DB name was inaccurate — actual DBs listed above.

Docker infrastructure running via docker-compose.infra.yml. Containers: propertize-mongodb (27017, healthy), propertize-redis (6379, healthy), propertize-kafka (9092, healthy), propertize-zookeeper (2181), propertize-kafka-ui (8090), propertize-mongo-express (8089). Health checks fixed: MongoDB uses mongosh with 30s timeout + 30s start_period; Redis uses redis-cli ping; Kafka uses kafka:29092 internal port with 30s timeout + 60s start_period. Run with: docker compose -f docker-compose.infra.yml up -d

Validates: all Docker containers, all port connectivity (13 services), Spring Boot actuator health, Eureka registrations, Redis/MongoDB/Kafka/PostgreSQL connectivity, and Frontend HTTP status.

FIXED TokenController.java and EnhancedJwtTokenProvider.java in api-gateway on 2026-03-16. Bugs fixed: (1) COMPILATION FAILURE — 8 missing methods added to EnhancedJwtTokenProvider: isAccessToken, isRefreshToken, isServiceToken, validateRefreshToken, validateServiceToken, getServiceName, getTargetService, getServiceScopes. (2) 7-parse fan-out eliminated in /token/validate — now parses Claims ONCE via getClaims(), added extractRolesFromClaims(Claims) and getTokenType(Claims) helpers to provider. (3) isEmpty() replaced with isBlank() for whitespace-only token input. (4) Bearer prefix stripping added via stripBearer() helper in controller. (5) @lombok.Builder (fully-qualified) replaced with @Builder + proper import. (6) Request/failure logging added via @Slf4j. (7) validateRefreshToken now distinguishes 'wrong token type' (400) from 'expired/invalid signature' (200 valid=false). (8) Server exceptions now return 500 instead of 200 OK. (9) generateAccessToken now sets explicit type=access claim for backward compat. All tests pass: EnhancedJwtTokenProviderTest, CrossServiceAuthenticationIT.

DEEP ANALYSIS AND BUG FIX SESSION 2026-03-16. All 6 services restored to full health. BUGS FIXED: (1) CRITICAL — api-gateway/application-local.yml ALL route ports were wrong: auth→8084(nothing), propertize→8081(auth port), employee→8082(propertize port). Fixed to auth→8081, propertize→8082, employee→8083, wagecraft→8084(placeholder). (2) CRITICAL — start-all-local.sh had bash syntax error: 'waitif' orphan in step 5 (missing wait_for_service 'Employee Service' 8083 + orphaned build block). Fixed and restored. (3) CRITICAL — start-all-local.sh step 6 used '$JAVA_HOME/bin/mvn' (Maven is NOT in JAVA_HOME). Fixed to use ./mvnw || mvn. (4) CRITICAL — start-all-local.sh did not source root .env before starting Java services, so REDIS_PASSWORD was never available to auth-service. Added 'set -a; source .env; set +a' block at top. (5) BUG — auth-service/application-local.yml Redis password was empty; Docker Redis requires 'redis_secure_pass'. Fixed to ${REDIS_PASSWORD:redis_secure_pass}. (6) BUG — propertize/application-local.yml Eureka URL missing credentials (http://localhost:8761/eureka/); fixed to http://admin:admin@localhost:8761/eureka/ for consistency. (7) BUG — api-gateway JAR was stale (Feb 15) — rebuilt after TokenController changes. SERVICE STATUS AFTER FIX: All UP — Service Registry:8761, Auth:8081, Propertize:8082, Employee:8083, API Gateway:8080, Frontend:3000. Login test via gateway PASSED.

TWO REDIS INSTANCES observed: (1) Homebrew Redis v8.4.0 at localhost:6379 without password (config: /opt/homebrew/etc/redis.conf). (2) Docker Redis v7-alpine container (propertize-redis) also mapped to 0.0.0.0:6379. Java services connect to Homebrew Redis (wins the port binding). auth-service connects to local Redis without password — this works but is not ideal. Recommendation: either stop Homebrew Redis ('brew services stop redis') so Docker Redis is used, or set REDIS_PASSWORD='' in .env for local dev consistency. Python file: only file in project is node_modules/flatted/python/flatted.py — it's an npm dependency, not a project file. No project-specific Python scripts exist.

Frontend (Next.js) was down because: (1) auth-service was down so no logins possible, (2) api-gateway was down so all API calls failed, (3) the process had died (PID in .pids/frontend.pid was dead). Frontend .env.development has VITE_ prefix variables — these are for Vite and are IGNORED by Next.js. Active env files in priority order: .env.local (overrides all, has API_URL=http://localhost:8080 — correct), .env.development (Vite vars, irrelevant), .env (Docker URLs - only used when .env.local absent). Frontend health check: use http://localhost:3000/ or http://localhost:3000/login (returns 200), NOT /actuator/health (returns 404 — Next.js doesn't have actuator).

Bruno collection at propertize/bruno-collection/ is UP TO DATE. Contains 37 folders (01-Authentication through 37-Approval-Workflow) + environments/ + bruno.json. Last updated per memo: 2026-03-16. Also contains a stray Edureka.bru file at root level — this appears to be a test file that should be removed or moved.

Local dev auth credentials: username=admin, password=password (bcrypt hash confirmed). Login endpoint: POST http://localhost:8080/api/v1/auth/login via API Gateway, or POST http://localhost:8081/api/v1/auth/login direct. Response: { accessToken, refreshToken, tokenType='Bearer', expiresIn=900, username, roles }. accessToken expires in 15 min. Use 'Authorization: Bearer <accessToken>' header for all protected endpoints. Auth DB: propertize_auth (PostgreSQL, user: ravishah). Users table has 2 users: admin (admin@propertize.com) and ravishah (ravi@test.com).

JWT KEY MISMATCH FIX (2026-03-17): Root cause of all 401 errors was 3 separate RSA key sets across services. CANONICAL key = auth-service/keys/ (Key Set A, fingerprint: abc47cc319a93d3b). FIXES APPLIED: (1) propertize/src/main/resources/application-local.yml — default key path was './propertize/config/keys/' (wrong: adds extra 'propertize/' prefix when WD=propertize/). Fixed to './config/keys/private_key.pem' and './config/keys/public_key.pem'. (2) employee-service/src/main/resources/application-local.yml — default key path was './auth-service/keys/public_key.pem' (wrong when WD=employee-service/). Fixed to '../auth-service/keys/public_key.pem'. (3) auth-service/src/main/resources/config/keys/ — was Key Set B. Replaced with Key Set A. security.rsa.* paths changed from classpath: to file paths. (4) api-gateway/src/main/resources/keys/public_key.pem — was Key Set C. Replaced with Key Set A. jwt.rsa.* added to application-local.yml. (5) employee-service/config/keys/public_key.pem — was Key Set B. Replaced with Key Set A. All 3 services rebuilt and restarted. JWT validation now works end-to-end.

PROPERTIZE DB USER SEED (2026-03-17): propertize_db.users table was empty — no users existed so all authenticated endpoints returned 404 'User not found'. The User entity in propertize is @Immutable read-mirror of auth-service users, but stored in separate DB (propertize_db, user: dbuser). Created init-propertize-users.sql to seed admin (PLATFORM_OVERSIGHT + PLATFORM_OPERATIONS) and ravishah (ORGANIZATION_OWNER) users. Run: psql -h localhost -U ravishah -d propertize_db -f init-propertize-users.sql

LOGGING OVERHAUL + LOGIN FIX SESSION (2026-03-17). ROOT CAUSE OF LOGIN FAILURE: (1) auth.ts authorize() function used logger.error() which is silent on server-side (only saves to localStorage — unavailable in Node.js SSR). Backend 401s were invisible. (2) ravishah account was rate-limited to locked state after 5 failed attempts (Redis lockout — expired naturally). FIXES APPLIED: (A) propertize-front-end/src/auth.ts — added console.log/console.error calls throughout authorize() for server-side visibility; removed mode:'cors' from server-side fetch (unnecessary on SSR). (B) propertize-front-end/src/services/logger.service.ts — ALL log methods (debug/info/warn/error/critical) now write to process.stdout/stderr server-side AND console.*/console.error client-side. 6 new LogCategory values added: PAYMENT, NOTIFICATION, DOCUMENT, SEARCH, ORGANIZATION, USER. (C) Logger import added to all 20+ frontend services: organization, maintenance, notification, search, rental-application, document, audit, lease, dashboard, user, tenant, auth.service, payment, property. (D) console.* replaced with logger calls in: auth.service.ts (logout/session/token methods), payment.service.ts (create/process/refund debug+info+error), property.service.ts (transform error handler). (E) propertize/src/main/java/com/propertize/session/SessionManagementService.java — FIXED UserSessionInfo cross-service deserialization crash: cleanupExpiredSessions() and getUserSessions() now use per-key try-catch for SerializationException. Auth-service sessions (@class=com.propertize.platform.auth.dto.UserSessionInfo) are skipped gracefully instead of crashing the entire cleanup batch. Propertize-core rebuilt (218MB JAR), running as PID 18762, STATUS=UP, no serialization errors. Login confirmed working: POST /api/v1/auth/login returns accessToken for admin/password.

FRONTEND CRASH FIX (2026-03-17): Post-login 'Maximum call stack size exceeded' crash fixed. ROOT CAUSE: LoggerService.interceptConsole() in propertize-front-end/src/services/logger.service.ts overrides console.error/warn/info to capture all logs. In the previous session, the modified error()/warn()/info()/critical() methods called console.error/console.warn/console.info — creating an infinite mutual recursion: intercepted console.error → this.error() → console.error() → intercepted console.error → ... → stack overflow. The crash appeared as 'unhandledRejection: RangeError: Maximum call stack size exceeded' in browser. SYMPTOM: Page became unresponsive after login; session data appeared empty in dev logs. FIX: In error(), warn(), info(), critical() methods — replaced console.error/warn/info client-side calls with 'this.originalConsole.error ?? console.error' (and same for warn/info). Also fixed loadLogsFromStorage() catch block. These use the stored original console methods (captured before override) bypassing the intercepted versions, breaking the recursion chain. Fix hot-reloaded by Turbopack in 1800ms. Login flow verified working: POST /login 200 → session populated → dashboard accessible.

LOG ANALYSIS SESSION (2026-03-17): Analyzed frontend app-logs and backend propertize-error.log. FIXES APPLIED: (1) PaymentScheduler crash — retryFailedPayments() called getAllPayments() which reads OrganizationContext ThreadLocal → null in @Scheduled tasks. Added PaymentService.getFailedPaymentsForSystemRetry(Pageable) that queries repo directly. Updated PaymentScheduler to call this method. (2) Frontend no-org warning — httpClient.ts spammed warning for PLATFORM_* admin users who legitimately have no org. Added check: if (user.role.startsWith('PLATFORM_')) skip warning. (3) Token refresh HTTP 500 — propertize-front-end/src/app/api/auth/refresh/route.ts called ${backendUrl}/api/auth/refresh (missing /v1/ segment) → backend 404 → catch block returned 500. Fixed to /api/v1/auth/refresh. (4) Optimistic lock 400 on concurrent updates — AdminOrganizationManagementController.assignApplicationReviewer() generic catch(Exception) returned 400 for ObjectOptimisticLockingFailureException. Added specific catch block returning HTTP 409 Conflict.

AUTHSERVICECLIENT URL BUG (2026-03-17): AuthServiceClient.buildUrl() was serializing Object[] args as its toString() value (e.g., '%5BLjava.lang.Object;@54192864') into the URL path. ROOT CAUSE: String.format(template, apiVersion, args) where args=Object[] — Java treats it as a single Object argument (array), calling args.toString(). FIX: Rebuilt allArgs array manually: Object[] allArgs = new Object[1 + args.length]; allArgs[0] = apiVersion; System.arraycopy(args, 0, allArgs, 1, args.length); return String.format(template, allArgs). File: propertize/src/main/java/com/propertize/client/AuthServiceClient.java.

STARTUP CRASH FIX — ClassNotFoundException: ErrorResponse (2026-03-17): SecurityExceptionHandler.java imported com.propertize.response.ErrorResponse but the class lived at com.propertize.dto.common.ErrorResponse with wrong package declaration (com.propertize.response). FIXES: (1) ErrorResponse.java — changed package declaration from response to dto.common. (2) SecurityExceptionHandler.java — updated import to com.propertize.dto.common.ErrorResponse. Also fixed: OrganizationMembership.java had @Index(name=idx_membership_roles, columnList=roles) on organization_membership table — but 'roles' is an @ElementCollection stored in a separate membership_roles table. Removed the invalid @Index. These two together eliminated all FATAL/WARN startup errors.

TEST CONFIG CONSOLIDATION (2026-03-17): Deleted propertize/src/test/resources/application-test.properties and merged all its unique values into application-test.yml (now the single canonical test config). Values merged: spring.main.allow-bean-definition-overriding=true, spring.data.redis.host/port (localhost:6379), spring.flyway.enabled=false, spring.jpa.hibernate.ddl-auto=none (changed from create-drop — PostgreSQL-specific DDL incompatible with H2), service.authentication.enabled=false, employecraft.api.url=http://localhost:8083, cors.allowed-origins (localhost:3000/3001/8080). The YAML (with spring.cloud.discovery.enabled and loadbalancer.enabled already present) is now complete.

ORGANIZATIONCONTEXTSERVICE BUILD FIX (2026-03-17): Removed unnecessary @Autowired annotation from OrganizationContextService constructor and its unused import (org.springframework.beans.factory.annotation.Autowired). Spring Boot 3.x auto-detects single-constructor injection — @Autowired is redundant and caused a compile warning treated as error.

PROPERTIZE RUNTIME FIXES (2026-03-17): Three issues fixed in propertize-core. (1) RSA KEY PATH for IntelliJ — application-local.yml 'tracing.rsa.*' and 'jwt.rsa.*' default paths changed from './config/keys/private_key.pem' to './propertize/config/keys/private_key.pem'. Root cause: When running from IntelliJ, CWD is the project root '/propertize-Services/', not the 'propertize/' subdirectory. Shell scripts (start-all-local.sh) explicitly pass -DPRIVATE_KEY_PATH= so are unaffected. (2) DUPLICATE INDEX idx_event_type — Both StoredEvent (event_store table) and ScheduleEvent (schedule_events table) declared @Index(name='idx_event_type'). PostgreSQL index names are schema-scoped (global), causing 'relation already exists' on every restart. Fixed by renaming ScheduleEvent's index to 'idx_schedule_event_type'. Since ddl-auto=update, Hibernate auto-creates the new index on next restart. (3) IMMUTABLE USER UPDATE — UserQueries.DISABLE_USERS_BY_IDS used JPQL 'UPDATE User u SET u.enabled = false WHERE u.id IN :ids' but User entity is @Immutable. Changed to native SQL 'UPDATE users SET enabled = false WHERE id IN (:ids)' and updated UserRepository.disableUsersByIds() to use nativeQuery=true.

CROSS-DB FK VIOLATIONS FIXED (2026-03-18): Removed ALL @ManyToOne User associations from propertize-core entities that were creating FK constraints in propertize_db pointing to propertize_db.users. Root cause: Hibernate ddl-auto=update auto-creates FK constraints for @ManyToOne @JoinColumn declarations. New users (org owners, tenants, staff) are created exclusively in propertize_auth.users (auth-service DB); propertize_db.users only has seeded users (admin, ravishah). Any save of a record referencing a dynamic user triggered a PostgreSQL FK violation. ENTITIES FIXED: (1) Invoice.java — createdByUser (@ManyToOne) → Long createdByUserId (@Column). (2) LeaseAmendment.java — createdByUser + approvedByUser (@ManyToOne) → Long createdByUserId + Long approvedByUserId. (3) Property.java — removed @ManyToOne User listingAgent (PropertyListingAgentInfo embedded class already manages Long listingAgentId — no duplication). (4) PropertyManagement.java — assignedManager (@ManyToOne) → Long assignedManagerId. (5) Payment.java — owner (@ManyToOne) → Long ownerId + String ownerUsername; processedByUser (@ManyToOne with cascade=PERSIST+MERGE on @Immutable entity — CRITICAL guaranteed Hibernate exception) → Long processedByUserId + String processedByUsername. (6) SupportTicket.java — createdBy + assignedTo (@ManyToOne) → Long+String denormalized fields for each. FLYWAY: V10__Drop_User_FK_Constraints.sql created — uses PL/pgSQL DO$$ block to dynamically discover and DROP all FK constraints from invoice, payment, lease_amendment, support_tickets, property_management, property tables that reference users. Both services compile: BUILD SUCCESS.

REPOSITORIES, MAPPERS, SERVICES UPDATED FOR USER FK REMOVAL (2026-03-18): All call sites updated after removing @ManyToOne User from entities. REPOSITORIES: (A) SupportTicketRepository — findByCreatedBy_Id() → findByCreatedByUserId(); findByAssignedTo_Id() → findByAssignedToUserId(); JPQL count query t.assignedTo.id → t.assignedToUserId; countByAssignedToAndStatusIn → countByAssignedToUserIdAndStatusIn. (B) PaymentRepository — removed 'owner' from @EntityGraph(attributePaths); findByOwnerId()/findByOwnerIdAndPropertyId() with JPQL p.owner.id → findByOwnerUsername()/findByOwnerUsernameAndPropertyId() with p.ownerUsername (also fixed type mismatch: was comparing Long p.owner.id to String param). MAPPER: PaymentDtoMapper — processedByUser.username → processedByUsername (direct field); removed stale @Mapping(target='owner', ignore=true) and @Mapping(target='processedByUser', ignore=true) lines. SERVICES: (A) SupportTicketService — replaced UserRepository with AuthServiceClient; createTicket uses authServiceClient.getUserByUsername() setting createdByUserId+createdByUsername; updateTicket/getMyTickets/getAssignedTickets all updated to use authServiceClient and new field names. (B) PaymentContextService — replaced UserRepository with AuthServiceClient; all 3 processedByUser set-calls use authServiceClient.getUserByUsername() setting processedByUserId+processedByUsername; processOwnerPayout uses authServiceClient for owner lookup setting ownerId+ownerUsername; getOwnerPayouts/getOwnerPayoutsByProperty use renamed repository methods.

PASSWORD RESET SERVICE FIXED + AUTH-SERVICE PASSWORD ENDPOINT ADDED (2026-03-18): PasswordResetService.resetPassword() in propertize-core was calling userRepository.save(user) after user.setPassword(passwordEncoder.encode(newPassword)) on an @Immutable User entity — guaranteed Hibernate UnsupportedOperationException at runtime. DESIGN FIX: Password management must be owned exclusively by auth-service (single source of truth for credentials). CHANGES: (1) auth-service UserManagementService.java — added updatePassword(Long userId, String newPassword): fetches user, encodes with passwordEncoder.encode(), saves to propertize_auth DB. (2) auth-service UserManagementController.java — added PUT /api/v1/users/{id}/password endpoint; protected by ServiceAuthenticationFilter (requires X-Service-Api-Key + X-Service-Name headers). (3) propertize AuthServiceClient.java — added USER_PASSWORD_ENDPOINT constant ('/api/%s/users/%d/password') and updateUserPassword(Long userId, String newPassword) method with Resilience4J circuit breaker + retry. (4) propertize PasswordResetService.java — added AuthServiceClient injection; removed PasswordEncoder injection; replaced user.setPassword()+userRepository.save() with authServiceClient.updateUserPassword(user.getId(), newPassword). Password changes now flow: propertize-core PasswordResetService → auth-service PUT /users/{id}/password → UserManagementService → propertize_auth DB.

SQL COLUMN BAD GRAMMAR FIX (2026-03-20): BadSqlGrammarException 'column prop.street does not exist' — Hibernate Address @Embeddable creates address_street/address_city/address_state/address_zip/address_country columns. Four SQL files had wrong bare column names. FIXED: DashboardService.java (prop.street, prop.city, prop.state, prop.zip → prop.address_street, prop.address_city, prop.address_state, prop.address_zip), ReportService.java (same 4), ManagerDashboardService.java (same), TechnicianDashboardQueries.java (same). Backend compiles clean after fixes.

FRONTEND COMPILATION ERRORS FIXED (2026-03-20): 40+ TypeScript errors cleared. Root causes: (1) LoggerService overload mismatch — 20+ call sites used (category, message, data?) 3-arg form but service only had (message, data?) 2-arg overloads. Added LogCategory overload signatures to all log methods (debug/info/warn/error/critical). (2) Unused imports — many services had imported but unused symbols, causing TS strict errors. Removed all unused imports across ~8 files. (3) errorHandler arg order — some files had (message, error) swapped. Fixed to match (error, message) signature. Result: npx next build → ✓ Compiled successfully in 24.0s.

TENANT LIST PAGE FIX (2026-03-20): /dashboard/tenants showed no data. ROOT CAUSE: Backend TenantResponse uses NESTED structure (basicInfo.firstName, basicInfo.lastName, contact.email, contact.phone, basicInfo.status, employment.employerName, employment.monthlyIncome) but page.tsx was accessing FLAT fields (tenant.firstName, etc.) → all undefined. The filter undefined?.toLowerCase()?.includes('') returns undefined (falsy), causing ALL tenants to be filtered out even with empty search. FIXES (8 replacements): (1) Filter guard: added !searchTerm || short-circuit. (2) Filter logic: tenant.basicInfo?.firstName || tenant.firstName etc. (3) Stats: added getTenantStatus() helper reading basicInfo?.status || tenantStatus || status. (4) All 4 table column renders updated to use nested field paths. (5) Action router.push paths now use (tenant.basicInfo?.id || tenant.id). File: propertize-front-end/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/tenants/page.tsx.

USERNAME → REAL NAME DISPLAY FIX (2026-03-20): Dashboard and rental application timelines showed raw usernames (e.g. OWN-kjhkjh) instead of first/last names. BACKEND CHANGES: (1) SecurityUtils.java — added getDisplayName(String username): queries UserRepository.findByUsername(), returns firstName+lastName or falls back to username. (2) MaintenanceRequestResponse.java — added submittedByName, assignedToName, submittedBy fields. (3) MaintenanceRequestMapper.java — converted interface→abstract class, added @Autowired SecurityUtils, added @Mapping for createdBy→submittedBy, added @AfterMapping resolveDisplayNames() calling securityUtils.getDisplayName() for both createdBy and assignedTo. (4) RentalApplicationWorkflowDTO.java — added submittedByName, reviewedByName, approvedByName, rejectedByName fields. (5) RentalApplicationService.java — added buildResponse() wrapper (calls RentalApplicationResponse.from() then enrichWorkflowDisplayNames()), enrichWorkflowDisplayNames() sets *ByName fields from resolveDisplayName() (UserRepository lookup). All 5 RentalApplicationResponse.from() call sites replaced with buildResponse(). FRONTEND CHANGES: DashboardWidgets.tsx (assignedToName in interface + render), EnhancedHorizontalTimeline.tsx + HorizontalTimeline.tsx (added *ByName props, updated decisionBy logic), rental-applications/[id]/page.tsx (passes *ByName fields), rental-application.types.ts (added *ByName to ApplicationWorkflowDTO). Both compile clean.

LEASE FLOW BUGS FIXED (2026-03-20): Investigation of what happens when lease is signed / tenant created revealed 3 critical bugs. (1) WELCOME EMAIL NEVER SENT: sendTenantOnboardingEmailAsync() had notificationService.sendTenantWelcomeEmail() commented out with wrong method signature. FIXED: Uncommented and corrected to sendTenantWelcomeEmail(tenant.getEmail(), tenant.getFirstName(), user.getUsername(), organizationId) — 4-arg signature. (2) REJECTION EMAIL NEVER SENT: sendRejectionNotificationAsync() had a TODO comment with no actual call. FIXED: Implemented using notificationService.sendEmailNotification(applicantEmail, subject, body, NotificationTypeEnum.LEASE_EXPIRED, null, null). (3) PROPERTY NEVER RETURNS TO AVAILABLE AFTER LEASE TERMINATION: updateRelatedEntityStatuses() checked lease.getStatus() == ACTIVE AFTER lease.setStatus(newStatus) was already called — so the ACTIVE check always used the new status. FIXED: Added previousStatus parameter to method signature, call site now passes currentStatus captured before setStatus(). Method condition changed to if (previousStatus == LeaseStatusEnum.ACTIVE). File: LeaseService.java. TENANT CREATION FLOW: Confirmed that approveApplication() → findOrCreateTenant() creates Tenant entity + calls authServiceClient.createUser() to create auth-service user — credentials now properly emailed after fix #1.

DEEP GAP ANALYSIS COMPLETED (2026-03-20): Comprehensive audit of entire project. TOP 10 PRIORITIES: (1) Document storage — DocumentService.generatePlaceholderUrl() returns fake paths; no MinIO/S3 integration; documents cannot actually be stored or retrieved. (2) Stripe disabled by default — stripe.enabled=false in application-local.yml; all payment operations fail silently; refund logic broken (returns null gateway response). (3) Dashboard stubbed — DashboardService, ManagerDashboardServiceImpl, TechnicianDashboardServiceImpl have TODO stubs for most queries; GraphQL resolvers return empty/null. (4) Reports page — frontend reports page has 100% hardcoded mock data array; never calls ReportService backend. (5) Platform Oversight dashboard — 100% hardcoded mock data; no API calls. (6) Employee service gateway routing — employee-service has NO routes in api-gateway GatewayRouteConfig.java; all employee endpoints unreachable through gateway. (7) CRUD forms missing — Assets, Vendors, Expenses create/edit forms all show 'coming soon' toast; no actual forms implemented. (8) Password change broken on frontend — profile page has changePassword UI but never calls API (AuthServiceClient changePassword was removed). (9) Analytics fire-and-forget — AnalyticsService.trackEvent() writes to DB but never aggregates efficiently; analytics dashboard endpoints return empty lists. (10) TechnicianDashboardQueries references columns that don't exist (scheduled_date, estimated_hours on maintenance_request table). ADDITIONAL GAPS: No Showing entity (showings managed as strings), no Rating/Review entity, no Inventory entity, no recurring payment scheduling, notification preferences UI incomplete, lease renewal flow missing, invoice PDF generation stubbed.

INVOICE CREATE LEASEID BUG FIXED (2026-03-20): Invoice create form (/dashboard/invoices/create) was using formData.propertyId as the leaseId in the submission (with a TODO comment), but InvoiceCreateRequest has @NotBlank leaseId — submissions always failed validation. FIXES: (1) Added leaseService import. (2) Replaced propertyId field with leaseId in formData state. (3) Added leaseLoading state. (4) handleChange now calls fetchActiveLease(tenantId) when tenantId changes. (5) fetchActiveLease() calls leaseService.getLeasesByTenant(tenantId), finds first ACTIVE lease (or first available), extracts basicInfo?.id || id, sets formData.leaseId. (6) Added validation: if leaseId is empty after tenant selection, block submission with clear error message. (7) Fixed Tenant interface from flat fields to nested structure (basicInfo?, contact?) matching actual API response. (8) Fixed dropdown render to use basicInfo?.firstName || firstName etc. (9) Added visual indicators: 'Fetching active lease...' spinner text, green 'Active lease found.' or amber 'No active lease found for this tenant.' messages. Frontend build: ✓ Compiled successfully in 23.2s. File: propertize-front-end/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/invoices/create/page.tsx.

COMPILATION FIXES (2026-03-20): (1) MaintenanceRequestMapper.java — PRE-EXISTING bug: extra '}' at line 134 closed the class prematurely, orphaning toResponse/toResponseList/toSummaryResponse/toEntity/updateEntityFromRequest/formatAddress methods outside class body. Removed duplicate block (lines 135-234). (2) ReportService.java — 'import com.propertize.dto.*' failed because dto/ package has no direct Java files (only subpackages). Removed unused wildcard import. (3) RentalApplicationService.java — referenced NotificationTypeEnum.LEASE_EXPIRED which doesn't exist. Changed to LEASE_EXPIRATION. All 3 services now compile clean: propertize (./mvnw compile -q), auth-service (mvn compile -q), api-gateway (./mvnw compile -q). Frontend: npm run build → ✓ Compiled successfully.

SPRING BOOT ARCHITECTURE AUDIT (2026-03-20): Full code quality scan across all 5 Java services. FINDINGS: (1) FIELD INJECTION: Only found in test files (acceptable). Production code uses constructor injection ✅. (2) @TRANSACTIONAL: Correctly applied to service methods only ✅. (3) CIRCULAR DEPS: No actual circular dependencies confirmed. (4) SEPARATION OF CONCERNS: HttpServletRequest in SessionManagementService/ContactUsService is justified for session/audit purposes. (5) EXCEPTION HANDLING: TechnicianDashboardService properly throws ServiceException or returns fallbacks. AnalyticsService uses log.warn for async fire-and-forget (acceptable). RbacService returns false on auth check failures (fail-closed, correct pattern). (6) SQL: All JdbcTemplate queries use parameterized ? placeholders ✅. FIXES APPLIED: (A) TIMING ATTACK — ServiceAuthenticationFilter.java used String.equals() for API key validation. Changed to MessageDigest.isEqual() for constant-time comparison. (B) PASSWORD LOGGING — DefaultUserInitializer.java logged plaintext password on user creation. Removed the log line. (C) MISSING EXCEPTION HANDLER — auth-service had no @RestControllerAdvice. Created GlobalExceptionHandler.java handling: MethodArgumentNotValidException (400), BadCredentialsException (401), AuthenticationException (401), AccessDeniedException (403), EntityNotFoundException (404), IllegalArgumentException (400), generic Exception (500). Returns consistent JSON: {success, status, message, timestamp, details?}.

PROPERTIZE BUILD FIXES (2026-03-22): Three compilation issues fixed — all via clean 'mvnw clean package -DskipTests' with BUILD SUCCESS. (1) TenantRepository.java: Method 'findByOrganizationIdAndTenantStatus(String, TenantStatusEnum, Pageable)' returning Page<Tenant> was missing its @Query annotation and method signature — only the @Param lines remained, causing a syntax error. Fixed by adding the full @Query + method declaration before the orphaned parameters. File: propertize/src/main/java/com/propertize/repository/TenantRepository.java. (2) LeaseService.java: LeaseController called leaseService.renewLease(String, LeaseRenewalRequest) which did NOT exist. Added the full renewLease() @Transactional method: validates lease can be renewed (not TERMINATED/CANCELLED), checks renewalInfo.canRenew() limit, extends end date from current end date by newDurationMonths, updates monthlyRent if provided, calls renewalInfo.recordRenewal(userId), sets status to RENEWED, saves, audit logs, and returns the updated Lease. Also added imports for LeaseRenewalRequest and LeaseRenewalInfo. (3) GraphQLAuthorizationInterceptor.java: The anonymousUser check used only name-based string match '"anonymousUser".equals(auth.getName())'. Added AnonymousAuthenticationToken instanceof check as belt-and-suspenders. Also added same check to buildPolicyContext() fallback. Import added: org.springframework.security.authentication.AnonymousAuthenticationToken.

GRAPHQL SECURITY FIX (2026-03-22): /graphql endpoint was listed in SecurityConfig PUBLIC_ENDPOINTS (line 98). This caused Spring Security to skip JWT validation for all GraphQL requests. Anonymous users reached the GraphQL resolvers, were caught by @AuthorizeGraphQL → GraphQLAuthorizationInterceptor, but the anonymousUser string check was being bypassed (possibly due to thread context propagation with WebGraphQlInterceptor). FIX: Removed '/graphql' from PUBLIC_ENDPOINTS — it now requires authentication at the Spring Security filter chain level. Kept '/graphiql' public (dev UI only). The interceptor-level check was also hardened with instanceof AnonymousAuthenticationToken. After the fix, unauthenticated /graphql calls return HTTP 401 from the filter chain before reaching any GraphQL resolver, eliminating the repeated 'GraphQL access denied for user anonymousUser on dashboard:read' WARN log spam.

PROFILE PAGE 403 FIX (2026-03-24): TENANT users hitting 'Access Denied: Required permission: user:read' on the profile page. ROOT CAUSE: profile/page.tsx called UserService.getUserById(userId) → GET /api/v1/users/{id} → routed by api-gateway to propertize-core → UserController.getUserById() has @Authorize(resource=USER, action=READ). TENANT role has no USER_READ permission. FIX STRATEGY: Rather than add USER_READ to TENANT (would allow reading any user — security risk), added GET /api/v1/auth/me to auth-service AuthController. This endpoint: (a) routes via existing /api/v1/auth/** gateway route → auth-service, no RBAC; (b) uses X-Username header injected by JwtAuthenticationFilter in api-gateway to identify the caller; (c) looks up user via userRepository.findByUsernameWithRoles(username); (d) returns id, username, email, firstName, lastName, phoneNumber, organizationId, organizationCode, roles, enabled, accountNonLocked, accountNonExpired, credentialsNonExpired. FRONTEND CHANGES: (1) user.service.ts — added static getMyProfile() calling GET /api/v1/auth/me, returns UserResponse directly (auth-service returns plain Map, not wrapped). (2) profile/page.tsx — removed useSession import and const data=useSession(); replaced loadProfile body to call UserService.getMyProfile() instead of getUserById(session.user.id). File: auth-service/.../controller/AuthController.java, propertize-front-end/src/services/user.service.ts, propertize-front-end/src/app/(dashboard)/dashboard/profile/page.tsx. NOTE: handleSavePersonalInfo still calls PUT /api/v1/users/{id} (USER_UPDATE) — if TENANT tries to save profile, a 403 may occur. Add PUT /api/v1/auth/me endpoint if profile editing for TENANT is needed.

TENANT CREDENTIAL WIRING (2026-03-24): When a lease is activated, auth-service login credentials are now automatically created for the tenant. ROOT CAUSE OF MISSING CREDENTIALS: LeaseService.updateRelatedEntityStatuses() did not call authServiceClient when transitioning to ACTIVE status. IMPLEMENTATION: (1) LeaseService.java — added fields: organizationRepository, authServiceClient, usernameGenerator. (2) updateRelatedEntityStatuses() — when newStatus=ACTIVE and tenant.getUserId()==null, calls new provisionTenantCredentials(lease, tenant) method. (3) provisionTenantCredentials(Lease, Tenant) — resolves org code from organizationRepository; generates username via usernameGenerator.generateUsername(UserRoleEnum.TENANT) (pattern RES-XXXXXXX); generates 12-char secure password (uppercase+lowercase+digit+special, shuffled); calls authServiceClient.createUser() with TENANT role and orgCode; sets tenant.setUserId(createdUser.getId()). Logs credentials prominently. Catches AuthServiceClient.AuthServiceException for already-exists scenario. (4) generateTenantPassword() — 12-char: 1+ uppercase, 1+ lowercase, 1+ digit, 1+ special, remainder random chars from full set, shuffled. IMPORTANT: For existing ACTIVE leases where tenant.userId==null, credentials were NOT retroactively created — run a backfill script or manually trigger. File: propertize/src/main/java/com/propertize/services/LeaseService.java.
